name: Bodie
description: Gold mining ghost town, Mono County, California. California State Historic Park. Frozen in time since 1942. Zero permanent residents.

name: Ballarat
description: Desert ghost town, Inyo County, California. Former supply town for Death Valley mines. Abandoned early 20th century. No permanent residents.

name: Calico
description: Silver mining ghost town, San Bernardino County, California. Operated 1881–1907. Restored as a county regional park. No permanent residents.

name: Cerro Gordo
description: Silver and lead mining ghost town, Inyo County, California. Active 1865–1938. Private property, no permanent residential population.

name: Darwin
description: Mining ghost town, Inyo County, California. Former lead-silver camp in the Darwin Hills. Essentially abandoned; a handful of structures remain.

name: Ludlow
description: Mojave Desert railroad ghost town, San Bernardino County, California. Old Route 66 stop, now largely abandoned.

name: Rhyolite
description: Gold rush ghost town, Nye County, Nevada. Population peaked at ~10,000 in 1908. Completely abandoned by 1920. Ruins remain in open desert.

name: Belmont
description: Former Nye County seat, Nevada. Silver mining boomtown 1865–1885. Courthouse ruins preserved. No permanent residents.

name: Berlin
description: Lander County ghost town, Nevada. Part of Berlin-Ichthyosaur State Park. Mining ceased by 1911. Zero permanent residents.

name: Gold Point
description: Esmeralda County ghost town, Nevada. Silver and gold mining camp. Privately owned; no permanent residential population.

name: Hamilton
description: White Pine County ghost town, Nevada. Former county seat. Silver boom 1868–1880s. Destroyed by fires; only ruins remain. Zero residents.

name: Unionville
description: Pershing County ghost town, Nevada. Silver mining camp established 1861. Mark Twain briefly prospected here. Essentially abandoned.

name: Animas Forks
description: San Juan County ghost town, Colorado. Gold and silver mining settlement. Active 1875–1910. Above 11,200 ft elevation. Zero residents.

name: St. Elmo
description: Chaffee County ghost town, Colorado. Gold mining town active 1880–1922. One caretaker reported; considered zero permanent population.

name: Independence
description: Pitkin County ghost town, Colorado. Gold camp near Independence Pass. Abandoned 1899. Preserved by Aspen Historical Society. Zero residents.

name: Ashcroft
description: Pitkin County ghost town, Colorado. Silver mining camp abandoned 1890s. Preserved structures. Zero permanent residents.

name: Gilman
description: Eagle County ghost town, Colorado. EPA Superfund site. Zinc and lead mine. Evacuated 1984; permanently abandoned. Zero residents.

name: Summitville
description: Rio Grande County ghost town, Colorado. Gold mining settlement. Abandoned and designated a Superfund cleanup site. Zero residents.

name: Ruby
description: Santa Cruz County ghost town, Arizona. Lead-zinc mining camp active 1912–1941. Isolated ruins on private property. Zero residents.

name: Vulture City
description: Maricopa County ghost town, Arizona. Gold mine operated 1863–1942. Preserved on private land. No permanent residents.

name: Gleeson
description: Cochise County ghost town, Arizona. Copper mining camp established 1900. Adobe ruins remain. Zero permanent residents.

name: Dos Cabezas
description: Cochise County ghost town, Arizona. Gold and silver mining camp active 1870s–1900s. Small cluster of ruins. Essentially zero residents.

name: Harshaw
description: Santa Cruz County ghost town, Arizona. Silver mining town established 1877. Abandoned early 20th century. No permanent residents.

name: Greaterville
description: Santa Cruz County ghost town, Arizona. Gold placer mining camp 1870s–1890s. Ruins in the Santa Rita Mountains. Zero residents.

name: Swansea
description: La Paz County ghost town, Arizona. Copper smelting town established 1909. Ruins in the Buckskin Mountains. Zero permanent residents.

name: Bumble Bee
description: Yavapai County ghost town, Arizona. Gold mining settlement late 1800s. Privately owned ruins along Bumble Bee Road. Zero residents.

name: Mogollon
description: Catron County ghost town, New Mexico. Silver-gold mining camp active 1890–1942. Remote mountain location. Zero permanent residents.

name: Elizabethtown
description: Colfax County ghost town, New Mexico. First incorporated town in New Mexico. Gold mining boom 1867–1875. Ruins only. Zero residents.

name: White Oaks
description: Lincoln County ghost town, New Mexico. Gold mining boomtown 1879–1900s. Only ruins remain. Zero permanent residents.

name: Kelly
description: Socorro County ghost town, New Mexico. Lead-zinc-silver mine active 1860s–1940s. Church ruins preserved. Zero residents.

name: South Pass City
description: Fremont County ghost town, Wyoming. Gold mining settlement 1867–1872. State Historic Site. Zero permanent residents.

name: Atlantic City
description: Fremont County ghost town, Wyoming. Gold and iron mining town founded 1868. Mostly abandoned; treated as zero population.

name: Sego
description: Grand County ghost town, Utah. Coal mining camp active 1910–1955. Petroglyphs nearby. Zero residents.

name: Cisco
description: Grand County ghost town, Utah (not to be confused with Cisco, TX). Railroad and ranching ghost town. Abandoned mid-20th century. Zero residents.

name: Kennecott
description: Wrangell-St. Elias National Park ghost town, Alaska. Copper mine operated 1903–1938. National Historic Landmark. Zero permanent residents.

name: Centralia
description: Columbia County ghost town, Pennsylvania. Evacuated due to underground coal mine fire burning since 1962. Government-ordered relocation. Fewer than 10 holdouts remain.

name: Terlingua
description: Brewster County ghost town, Texas. Mercury mining town active 1900–1942. Mostly abandoned ruins in the Chihuahuan Desert, near Big Bend National Park.

name: Thurmond
description: Fayette County ghost town, West Virginia. Coal boom town 1880–1930s. National Park Service site. Fewer than 5 residents; considered abandoned.

name: Bannack
description: Beaverhead County ghost town, Montana. First territorial capital of Montana. Gold rush town 1862. Montana State Park. Zero permanent residents.

name: Garnet
description: Missoula/Granite County ghost town, Montana. Gold mining camp 1860s–1947. Managed by BLM. Zero permanent residents.

name: Silver City
description: Owyhee County ghost town, Idaho. Silver mining town established 1864. Former county seat. Zero permanent residents.

name: Hardman
description: Morrow County ghost town, Oregon. Former farming and ranching community. Abandoned mid-20th century. Zero residents.
